world.description: “Iran is trying to hold the global economy hostage in the Strait of Hormuz. They must not prevail,” said British Foreign Minister Yvette Cooper, calling for an immediate and unconditional reopening of the waterway.

world.title: Exclusive: US intelligence assesses Iran maintains significant missile launching capability, sources say
world.description: Roughly half of Iran’s missile launchers are still intact and thousands of one-way attack drones remain in Iran’s arsenal despite the daily pounding by US and Israeli strikes against military targets over the past five weeks, according to recent US intelligence assessments, three sources familiar with the intel told CNN.

business.title: New microwave frying technique could make french fries much healthier
business.description: Scientists have discovered a way to make French fries less greasy without ruining their taste. By combining regular frying with microwave heating, they reduce the amount of oil absorbed during cooking. The key lies in pressure inside the food—microwaves help push oil out instead of letting it seep in. The result: faster cooking, lower fat, and fries that can still stay crispy.

business.title: Iran war could make beer and bottled water pricier for Indians

business.description: Visa (NYSE:V) has introduced six new AI powered tools to overhaul how credit card disputes are handled. The tools focus on automating case analysis, generating merchant responses, and providing real time insights across the payments chain. Visa aims to reduce fraud related losses and inefficiencies tied to manual, fragmented dispute workflows for issuers, merchants, and payment processors. business.description: The Minister of Energy and Green Transition, Dr John Abdulai Jinapor, has held a strategic meeting with key stakeholders in Ghana’s upstream petroleum sector to align priorities and refine plans for the industry’s development.

science.title: The Artemis Generation Begins! Artemis II Launches for the Moon
science.description: At 06:25 p.m. EDT (03:25 p.m. PDT) on April 1st, the Artemis II mission lifted off from the historic Launch Pad-39B at NASA's Kennedy Space Center in Florida. This mission will send astronauts on a ten-day journey around the Moon and will be the first crewed mission to venture beyond Low Earth Orbit (LEO) since the Apollo Era.

science.title: Cell lineage tracing reveals early‑segregated germline in plants
science.description: August Weismann's germ plasm theory of the late 19th century posited that only germ cells, e.g., sperm and egg cells in animals or pollen and ovule cells in plants, transmit genetic information to the next generation, and that somatic mutations represent an evolutionary dead end. This theory has been highly validated in animals, whose germ cells are formed and segregated early in the organism's development.

science.title: Engineered E. coli dependency may help contain microbes to defined areas
science.description: Take a typical fish out of the water and it won't live long. It gets the oxygen it needs from the water it swims in. In a similar way, scientists are exploring dependency as a method of controlling what microbes can do and where they can do it.
